image_prompt: SVG: speed-time graph, Speed / ms⁻¹ (0-70, ticks 10) vs Time / s (0-70, ticks 10), straight line gently declining from (0,61) to (60,55).

render_data.title: Velocity–time graph: train through the tunnel
render_data.areaReadout: Tunnel length = area under graph = ½(v₁ + v₂) × t = ½(61 + 55) × 60 = 3480 m
render_data.task: Read off the entry and exit speeds and the time, then find the tunnel length from the area under the line.

image_prompt: SVG: scatter graph Frequency / Hz (0-1800, ticks 200) vs 1/4L / m⁻¹ (0-6, ticks 1), six points (0.8,290),(1.0,343),(1.3,450),(1.7,572),(2.5,860),(5.0,1715) with a straight best-fit line through the origin.

render_data.title: Plot frequency against 1/4L
render_data.gradientReadout: gradient = speed of sound ≈ 344 m s⁻¹ (1715 / 5.0)
render_data.task: Drag the handle to plot the data point for the 0.40 m tube at (2.5, 860), then a straight best-fit line passes through the origin.

image_prompt: SVG diagram: streamlined high-speed maglev train levitating above an elevated guideway with support pillars, mountains behind. Labelled 'Magnets on train' and 'Magnets on tracks', with captions explaining repelling force raises the train and reduces friction for high speed.

SVG diagram: transformer with a 3D laminated iron core (rectangular ring), teal primary coil on the left limb and orange secondary coil on the right limb. Labels: Primary coil Np turns, Secondary coil Ns turns, primary current Ip, secondary current Is, primary voltage Vp, secondary voltage Vs, + and - terminals, 'Iron core'.

topic: Ice melting: pure vs salt water convection
image_prompt: SVG illustration: two glass tumblers side by side on a wooden surface. Left labelled 'Pure water' with red dye spread/mixing through the whole glass (strong convection) and a small mostly-melted ice cube low down. Right labelled 'Salt water' with red dye staying in a sharp layer near the top (weak convection) and an intact ice cube floating high.

SVG diagram: a pan flute made of nine bamboo tubes closed at one end, bound by two rails, increasing in length from left (short, high pitch) to right (long, low pitch). Dark open tops, dashed teal arrow showing air blown across the open tops.

SVG clean data table with shaded header. Columns: Length L / m, Frequency f / Hz, 4L / m, 1/4L / m⁻¹. Rows: 0.05,1715,0.2,5.0 ; 0.10,860,(blank highlighted pink),(blank highlighted pink) ; 0.15,572,0.6,1.7 ; 0.20,450,0.8,1.3 ; 0.25,343,1.0,1.0 ; 0.30,290,1.2,0.8. Two pink cells in the 0.10 row are the values the student must calculate.

SVG diagram: a T-shaped wooden bench with a metal wire (guitar string) clamped at the left end, running horizontally over a pulley clamped to the right edge of the bench, then hanging down to a stack of slotted 100 g masses (hanging masses). An electronic tuner / sound-level meter sits on a small side table. Labels: metal wire (guitar string), pulley, hanging masses, electronic tuner.
SVG illustration: a red handheld sound level meter tilted, with a black foam microphone on a stem, a dark digital screen reading 112 dB, and a grid of grey buttons. Labelled 'Sound level meter'.

topic: Speaker and sound meter on school field with distance scale
image_prompt: SVG scene: a black speaker on the left on a school field, a sound level meter standing at the 2 m mark of a horizontal distance scale (0 to 12 m), a dashed red drop-line from the scale to the meter, a school building (labelled SCHOOL) and trees on the right, a city skyline behind, and a 'Slider control' at the bottom. Labels: Distance / m, Speaker, Slider control.

render_data.title: Sound level meter on the school field

image_prompt: SVG 3D diagram: an open cardboard box divided by a vertical wall of stacked cardboard layers. On one side a buzzer (with wires) emits sound bursts; on the other side a red sound level meter measures the sound. Labels: Cardboard layers, Buzzer, Sound level meter.

SVG illustration: the planet Mars as a shaded rust-red sphere on a starfield, with a white polar ice cap, darker maria patches, craters, a canyon streak, atmospheric glow and terminator shading. Labelled 'Mars'.

topic: Mars missions facts panel and timeline
image_prompt: SVG infographic: a dark Facts/Missions panel showing Mars on the left and a 'Facts' text block about crewed Mars missions (cost up to $100 billion over 30-40 years, 6-8 month journey, private companies, Mars One $6 billion, SpaceX ~$10 billion per person), plus a country-flag key (China, EU, India, Japan, UAE, USA, Russia). Below, a horizontal timeline of missions to Mars from 1960 to 2020 with decade ticks, a highlighted '1998 Japan enters the race (Nozomi)' marker, and a successful/unsuccessful legend.
